title: Signed capability entitlement (v1alpha2)
description: Signed, capability-based document the Hub issues and the Runtime's embedded Entitlement Verifier checks LOCALLY (no mandatory network call). TARGET-STATE contract. The entitlement authorises Runtime capabilities; it is NOT a secret, carries NO business data, and grants NO access to data by itself. It is integrity-protected (Ed25519), not encrypted. It encodes edition + capabilities + package scopes + expiry/grace + nonce/revision + signature. It NEVER embeds plan names or prices.

allOf.$comment: organization_id binds a team/organization entitlement to its org; personal (single owner) never carries one. Enforces the description on metadata.organization_id.

organization_id.description: Present for team/organization editions; absent for personal (single owner).

revision.description: Monotonic revision; the verifier ignores a revision lower than the last accepted one (rollback protection).

nonce.description: Single-use random value binding this issuance; the verifier rejects a replay of a superseded document.

capabilities.description: Stable capability identifiers the Runtime activates. Personal may be an empty list (base capabilities are always free and are NOT gated by entitlement).

package_scopes.description: Registry lanes this entitlement unlocks. 'public'/'community' are always available and need not be listed.

seats.description: Soft capacity hints for additional users/instances. Enforcement is a product boundary in the Team Capability Pack, never a data-hostage lever.

signature.description: Ed25519 detached signature over the canonicalised document. Constrained to ed25519 so the Runtime verifies offline with a pinned Hub key; sigstore/age are rejected. trust_domain is required and must match spec.offline_verification.trust_domain.
